title: Mega Houndoom Battle Guide
summary: A [dark]/[fire] special sweeper whose [solar-power] plus sun pushes effective Special Attack past 300 — one [fire-blast] ends most special walls outright.
strengths: [solar-power] plus sun gives a 1.5x Special Attack multiplier, effective SpA past 300
Base 115 Speed clears most unboosted meta sweepers
[dark] and [fire] STAB coverage leaves almost nothing on the opposing team safe
weaknesses: [solar-power] chips 1/8 HP per turn, so long games are unwinnable
2x to Fighting, Ground, Rock, and Water weaknesses remain
Loses the [solar-power] buff the moment sun is cleared

recommendedMoves.reason: Picked over flamethrower because [solar-power] pushes into OHKO territory
Reliable [dark] STAB for consistent damage between clicks
Stacks on top of [solar-power] and sun for a triple-boosted sweep
Kills Fairy switch-ins like Azumarill and Clefable

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Houndoom bumps Special Attack to 140, Speed to 115, and locks in [solar-power], which gives an additional 1.5x multiplier in sun. Under sun, effective Special Attack blows past 300 and [fire-blast] starts one-shotting special walls that would normally require a 2HKO. This is the Mega to bring when the team needs a finisher that cannot be walled.

## Strengths
Solar-power plus sun is one of the strongest special damage multipliers in the game. [dark-pulse] keeps its flinch utility while hitting bulky Psychic and Ghost walls, and the base 115 Speed means Mega Houndoom outruns every [timid] 140-or-below Speed tier without investment. Mega Evolution also bumps defenses to 90/90 — enough bulk to survive one weak special hit and still set up [nasty-plot].

## Weaknesses & Answers
Solar-power burns 1/8 HP every turn, so there is no long-game plan — the sweep has to close in three or four turns. The 2x weaknesses to Fighting, Ground, Rock, and Water stay intact after Mega, and priority users with aqua-jet or mach-punch still revenge-kill Mega Houndoom cleanly. The instant Rain, Sand, or Snow replaces sun, [solar-power] falls off and [fire] moves lose their weather bonus simultaneously — weather warfare is the entire story.

## Recommended Moveset
[fire-blast] is the mandatory finisher specifically because [solar-power] pushes it over OHKO thresholds that flamethrower can't hit. [dark-pulse] is the reliable spam click for consistent damage between [fire-blast] risks. [nasty-plot] takes the third slot — triple-stacking [solar-power], sun, and +2 turns any neutral hit into a guaranteed kill. [sludge-bomb] handles the Fairy switch-ins (Azumarill, Clefable) that otherwise wall the [dark] STAB.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[houndoominite] is the only item, no alternatives. The ability swaps to [solar-power] on Mega Evolution — this is the whole case for Mega over base. Tera [fire] is the default, maximizing [fire-blast] OHKO lines. Tera [dark] is the alternative pick for late-game [dark-pulse] sweeps, especially into Dragon cores.

## Teammates & Synergy
A dedicated sun setter (Ninetales) is effectively mandatory — without sun, Mega Houndoom is barely stronger than the base form. A stealth-rock remover is the next non-negotiable slot because 25% per entry ends runs instantly. Grass or Electric partners to handle enemy Water threats make Mega Houndoom's entries safer. Avoid pairing with trick-room — this mon lives on high Speed, not low.

## Play Patterns
Lead with the sun setter, switch Mega Houndoom in on turn 2, click [nasty-plot] if the opponent hard-swaps, and start firing. The [solar-power] chip forces a three-to-four-turn kill plan — there is no late-game scenario where Mega Houndoom wins a slow grind. Tera the instant a 2x weakness is telegraphed, or the turn [fire-blast] needs one extra percent of STAB to confirm a KO.
